wizard.title: NOVUS-OS Installation
wizard.description: Complete setup in guided steps and start NOVUS-OS installation.

welcome.title: Welcome to NOVUS-OS Installer
welcome.body: We will guide you through installation mode, panel access, and security settings step by step.
welcome.heroBody: You can adjust all values while moving through the wizard. Start now and choose New Install or Restore.

mode.title: Choose installation mode
mode.body: Select whether this node is a fresh install or a restore from backup.
mode.freshBody: Prepare a clean panel deployment with databases, agent, and web stack.
mode.restoreBody: Restore panel configuration from a previously exported bundle.
mode.restoreHint: Restore settings are applied during installation.

restore.sourceBody: Choose where to load your panel backup from: URL or local file.
restore.bundleUploadHint: Choose a local panel backup file.
restore.bundlePayloadPlaceholder: Paste the full encrypted NOVUS export envelope here.
restore.importModeBody: After key validation, choose how backup data should be applied.
restore.keyBody: Upload the key file and enter its password. If your key is not encrypted, you can use manual key material or recovery phrase.
restore.keyMaterialPlaceholder: Paste key material if you do not use a key file.
restore.keyPasswordPlaceholder: Enter password to unlock key file
restore.recoveryPhrasePlaceholder: Paste the operator recovery phrase used for the export.
restore.keyNeeded: Key material or password is required

domain.title: Domain and certificate
domain.body: Set domain or IP access for panel login and choose SSL strategy.
domain.domainHelp: Enter the domain you want to use, for example: project.com. You can also use an alias like panel.your-domain.com. If a domain is not available yet, you can use the panel server IP.
domain.ipDetectedBody: When using direct IP access, it is recommended to enable Security Entrance for additional protection.
domain.ipDetectedNote: You can later switch to a domain and issue a certificate from panel settings.

securityEntrance.title: Security Entrance
securityEntrance.body: Hide panel access behind a secret path and optional dedicated port with brute-force protection limits.
securityEntrance.pathHelp: This is the secret URL segment required for panel entry.
securityEntrance.portPlaceholder: Leave empty to reuse the standard port
securityEntrance.portHelp: Optional separate port for panel entry. Leave empty to keep default.
securityEntrance.windowHelp: Time window used to count failed login attempts.
securityEntrance.attemptsHelp: Allowed login attempts within the rate-limit window before lockout.
securityEntrance.blockHelp: How long access is blocked after max attempts is reached.

letsencrypt.title: Let's Encrypt (Automatic)
letsencrypt.body: Issue and configure the certificate automatically during installation.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare SSL
cloudflare.body: Use DNS validation through Cloudflare with your API token.

custom.title: Custom certificate
custom.body: Provide your own certificate and private key for immediate use.

admin.title: Administrator profile
admin.body: Create the first administrator account. Login is auto-generated from email and can be edited manually.
admin.loginHint: By default login is derived from email before {'@'}, but you can override it.
admin.readinessBody: Validate the handoff that will be used for the first privileged login and panel bootstrap.
admin.passwordCopied: Administrator password generated and copied to clipboard.

database.title: Database settings
database.body: Set the MariaDB passwords for the system administrator and panel before migrations start.
database.footer: The installer prepares separate NOVUS OS, identity, and service desk databases.
database.passwordCopied: Database password generated and copied to clipboard.
database.summaryBody: These databases are created during installation.

security.title: Keys and KMS
security.body: Choose how to prepare the master key and, if needed, an external KMS.
security.footer: Key and KMS settings are applied by the installer during deployment.
security.hybridBody: The installer generates the master key and configures the default backend automatically.
security.manualBody: Provide the master key manually in hex or base64.
security.cloudflareBody: Use a Cloudflare Worker for master-key operations.
security.tpm2Body: Use TPM2 when the target server supports hardware key protection.
security.manualMaterialBody: Generate a key or paste an existing value.
security.cloudflareConfigBody: Provide the credentials and worker endpoint for Cloudflare KMS.
security.cloudflareTokenPlaceholder: Leave empty to reuse the SSL token when applicable
security.cloudflareReuseSsl: If left empty, the installer reuses the SSL Cloudflare token when available.
security.cloudflareAdvancedBody: Specify script, namespace, and route only when required.
security.cloudflareRoutePlaceholder: Optional route pattern such as kms.example.com/*
security.hint: The installer writes environment settings, master-key material, and optional shared secrets in a compatible layout.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.body: Save external providers now if they should be available on first start.
integrations.footer: Only enabled providers with complete required fields are sent in the installer payload.
integrations.catalogBody: Open the provider side menu, choose an integration, and configure it in the modal.
integrations.providerLibraryBody: Choose a provider from this side menu. The settings modal opens above this list.
integrations.enableBody: Only enabled providers are serialized into the setup request.
integrations.invalidProvider: Fill all required fields before enabling this provider.

telegram.title: Telegram Global Bot
telegram.description: Primary global Telegram bot for installer notifications and runtime alerts.

discordNotifications.title: Discord Global Bot
discordNotifications.description: Primary global Discord bot for notifications and operational messages.

google.title: Google OAuth
google.description: Preconfigure Google as an identity provider for operator sign-in.

github.title: GitHub OAuth
github.description: Preconfigure GitHub sign-in for teams already anchored on GitHub org identity.

githubCli.title: GitHub CLI Token
githubCli.description: Store GitHub Personal Access Token for GitOps, automation, and CLI tasks.

gemini.title: Gemini AI
gemini.description: Configure Google Gemini API key for panel AI integrations.

discord.title: Discord OAuth
discord.description: Enable Discord as an identity provider separate from notification transport.

cloudflare.title: Cloudflare Provider
cloudflare.description: Persist Cloudflare API credentials for DNS, edge, and worker-driven platform services.

steam.title: Steam
steam.description: Store Steam Web API credentials for future catalog or identity workflows.

smtp.title: SMTP Mail Relay
smtp.description: Prime outbound mail with a real SMTP relay instead of leaving panel mail transport for later.

novusAgent.title: NOVUS Agent
novusAgent.description: Configure NOVUS Agent installer URL and authorization header for agent bootstrap flows.

summary.targetPending: Will be set on Domain step
summary.adminPending: Will be set on Admin step

success.title: Installation finished successfully
success.body: The panel is installed and the secure sign-in URL is ready.

errors.localeLoad: Unable to load interface translations. English will be used instead.
errors.restoreDisabled: Restore mode requires a valid source and unlock material.
errors.restoreFileRead: Unable to read the selected restore file.
errors.clipboard: Unable to copy to clipboard in this browser context.
errors.integrationInvalid: Fill all required provider fields before saving.
errors.securityEntranceInvalid: Security Entrance path must be a valid non-reserved token when enabled.
